message:
style: rustfmt tidy across mvcc telemetry and vdbe track_r tests

Three purely cosmetic adjustments picked up by rustfmt after the
preceding Track E5.1 and TRACK_R commits landed:

- `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/lib.rs`: `pub mod write_coordinator;`
  and `pub mod writer_routing_telemetry;` are reordered into
  strict alphabetical order (`write_coordinator` before
  `writer_routing_telemetry`), and the matching `pub use`
  re-export blocks are reordered the same way so rustfmt stops
  rewriting them on every other commit.
- `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/writer_routing_telemetry.rs`: three
  `assert!(has_signal(WriterRoutingTelemetrySignal::...))` calls
  in the source-coverage unit tests are reflowed to break the
  long enum path onto its own line, and the
  `WRITER_ROUTING_TELEMETRY_SOURCES.iter().any(...)` chain in the
  ownership-lineage assertion is reflowed onto three lines.
- `crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`: the TRACK_R
  `track_r_log_metrics(...)` call inside the proptest
  two-pass-vs-three-pass test reflows
  `scratch_capacity_bytes.get().max(two_pass.borrow().capacity())`
  onto three lines.

No behavior change; `cargo fmt --check` passes cleanly against
all three files after this commit.
